name: Warm Upside-Down Cheesecakes with Blueberry Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons cornstarch dissolved in 1 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees and set rack to middle position.
2. In a small saucepan melt the butter over moderately high heat. Stir in the crumbs. Divide the mixture among four paper-lined muffin tins, patting to form a crust. Bake for 5 minutes. Let cool on a rack for 5 minutes.
3. In a bowl with an electric mixer beat together the cream cheese, sugar, vanilla, almond extract, and a pinch of salt until well combined. Beat in the egg until the batter is well combined. Divide the batter among the tins (the batter will fill the tins). Bake cakes for 20 minutes or until set. Let cool on a rack for 10 minutes.
4. While cheesecakes are baking, combine blueberries with the remaining sugar and water in a small heavy saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes. Stir in the cornstarch mixture. Add to the blueberries with lemon juice. Simmer the mixture, stirring, for 2 minutes.
5. Discard paper liners. Invert cheesecakes onto plates and spoon sauce around them.
Yield: 4 servings
